question.text: Suresh, a salaried individual aged 40, earns a gross salary of Rs. 12,00,000 in FY 2025-26. He opts for the old tax regime and claims deductions of Rs. 1,50,000 under Section 80C and Rs. 50,000 as standard deduction. What is his tax liability before cess?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Gross salary = Rs. 12,00,000. Less: standard deduction Rs. 50,000 and 80C Rs. 1,50,000. Total income = Rs. 10,00,000. Old regime slabs: Up to 2.5L = Nil; 2.5-5L = 2.5L × 5% = Rs. 12,500; 5-10L = 5L × 20% = Rs. 1,00,000. Total tax = Rs. 12,500 + Rs. 1,00,000 = Rs. 1,12,500. **The Trap:** Rs. 1,27,500 results from forgetting the standard deduction (taxable income becomes Rs. 10,50,000, extra 50,000 × 30% = Rs. 15,000 added). Rs. 97,500 comes from applying 10% instead of 20% on the 5-10L slab. Rs. 1,50,000 applies an incorrect flat rate. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A MFD exam tests basic understanding of how deductions reduce taxable income for fund recommendations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Under the old regime, standard deduction of Rs. 50,000 plus Section 80C deductions can significantly reduce tax outgo — always compute net taxable income after all eligible deductions before applying slabs.
